input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the approach:

### 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

```xml
<!-- Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Themes/DarkTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...>
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#1E1E1E</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#E0E0E0</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#4CC2FF</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

```xml
<!-- Themes/HighContrastTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary ...>
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#000000</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="Accent">#FFFF00</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

### 2. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public enum AppTheme { Light, Dark, HighContrast }

public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(AppTheme theme)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;

        // Remove the current theme dictionary (always the last one added)
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(theme switch
        {
            AppTheme.Dark         => new Themes.DarkTheme(),
            AppTheme.HighContrast => new Themes.HighContrastTheme(),
            _                     => new Themes.LightTheme(),
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("app_theme", theme.ToString());
    }

    public static AppTheme LoadSavedTheme()
    {
        var saved = Preferences.Get("app_theme", nameof(AppTheme.Light));
        return Enum.TryParse<AppTheme>(saved, out var t) ? t : AppTheme.Light;
    }
}
```

### 3. Apply the saved theme at startup

In `App.xaml.cs`:

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(ThemeService.LoadSavedTheme());
}
```

### 4. Bind UI elements with `DynamicResource`

`DynamicResource` (not `StaticResource`) is required so values update at runtime:

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label Text="Hello" TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
    <Button BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource Accent}" Text="Go" />
</ContentPage>
```

### 5. Wire up the settings page

```xml
<!-- SettingsPage.xaml -->
<Picker x:Name="ThemePicker" Title="Theme"
        SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>High Contrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object? sender, EventArgs e)
{
    var theme = ThemePicker.SelectedIndex switch
    {
        1 => AppTheme.Dark,
        2 => AppTheme.HighContrast,
        _ => AppTheme.Light,
    };
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

### Key points

| Concept | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| **`DynamicResource`** | Re-evaluates when the dictionary changes; `StaticResource` won't update. |
| **`MergedDictionaries`** | Keeps theme resources isolated and swappable. |
| **`Preferences`** | Persists the user's choice across app restarts. |

This gives you instant, app-wide theme switching with no page reloads.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed resource dictionaries in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes that can be switched at runtime from a settings page. This is a knowledge/guidance question, not a code change request on an existing repo. I'll provide a concise, practical answer.
